title: Functions, Callbacks, Context, and Memory Interfaces · C Contract Brief: Api Contract Documentation
summary: A plugin registry invokes callbacks after their contexts change ownership and one implementation uses an incompatible signature. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem api contract documentation increment in typed callback and context registry. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Document nullability, bounds, aliasing, mutation, ownership, lifetime, errors, and thread rules at declarations.
Leave out-parameters in documented states on success and every failure, with ownership transfer made explicit.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Api Contract Documentation · Theory Api Contract Documentation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 4733. Produce bounded C evidence to bind callback results to explicit context lifetime; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.
